title: Module 3 quiz: Creative is the new targeting

questions.prompt: Why is the hook the highest-leverage lever?

choices.text: Average Meta ad watch time is under 4 seconds — whether the user stops depends almost entirely on the first 1-3 seconds. Everything downstream (value prop, social proof, CTA) only happens if the hook earns it. Spread between top-performing and bottom-performing hooks in the same ad set is typically 2-3x. A strong hook can carry an average offer; a weak hook kills a great offer.

questions.explanation: First 2-3 seconds decides if the rest of the ad matters.

questions.prompt: Library of 12 ads, all featuring the founder, all 30s talking-head, all anti-aging angle. Performance plateau. Best move?

choices.text: Diversify on every axis — that's 12 variants of one ad, not a diversified library. Add UGC (real customers or creators producing UGC-style), different creators reaching different demos, formats beyond talking-head (demos, before/after, carousels), and new angles (acne, sensitive-skin, routine-simplifier). Goal is 8-12 ads varying on multiple dimensions. Most scaling accounts run a creator pool — 2-5 creators on retainer.

questions.explanation: Variety beats variants. Diversify creator, format, angle, tone, hook.

choices.text: Ad-set-level changes: significant budget shifts (>20% in 7 days), targeting changes (audiences/exclusions), optimization-event swaps, bid-strategy changes. Adding/pausing individual ads inside a healthy ad set does NOT trigger reset. So creative iteration at the ad level preserves the ad set's accumulated signal — the modern operating pattern is to keep ad sets stable and rotate creative inside them weekly.

questions.explanation: Ad-set-level edits reset learning; ad-level changes don't.

questions.prompt: Daily-one-ad launches are giving random results. Fix?

choices.text: Batch weekly: 3-5 new ads launched together on the same day, 2-3 worst-performing paused the same day. Single-ad daily launches starve new ads — Meta rotates impressions across the existing library and a lone new ad competes against incumbents that already have signal. Batched launches give new ads fair shared exposure and a clean comparison set; readable results in 5-7 days.

questions.explanation: Weekly batches of 3-5 in / 2-3 out, same day.

questions.prompt: Daily dashboard for an active account — which 6 metrics?

choices.text: Reach, reactions, comments, shares, saves, video plays.

Spend, CPA, ROAS, CTR, hook rate, frequency. These cover all three creative layers (stop / hold / convert) and surface the most common failure modes — rising frequency, falling hook rate, CPA drift, ROAS breach. Engagement metrics are diagnostics, not goals; ignore them on the daily view. Weekly view adds prior-week deltas on the same six.

questions.explanation: Six metrics: spend, CPA, ROAS, CTR, hook rate, frequency.
